tweet: welcome back to throwback thursday we have another 3 part thread for you today 1 3 ever wondered where the ultimate cereal monkey legend comes from meet coco the monkey the absolute icon of kellogg s coco pops or cocoa pops in some markets coco first swung onto our screens back in 1963 some say 1961 in early black and white us style spots but it was his 1986 redesign that turned him into a proper british breakfast superstar brown fur that cheeky blue cap with the big yellow c white t shirt proudly showing off his name and blue pants voiced with a proper cheeky british accent he d swing through the jungle belting out the legendary jingle i d rather have a bowl of coco pops the ads were pure 80s 90s gold jungle capers with his mates like crafty croc kylie kangaroo ozmelda the ostrich shortie the giraffe and hefty the hippo there were spoon toppers musical mugs monkey friends figures and enough merch to fill a cereal aisle sales went through the roof thanks to him and he became a nostalgic staple for generations of kids who d rather have coco pops than well anything else pure monkey magic but the story gets even more british when you look at the name drama in 1998 kellogg s tried to rebrand the cereal as choco krispies across the uk to line it up with their global range sales took a nosedive almost immediately so they let the nation decide in a massive public vote in 1999 over a million people dialled in or logged on and a whopping 92 voted to bring back the beloved coco pops name the change happened in may 1999 complete with tongue in cheek election style ads featuring screaming lord sutch as the returning officer classic british democracy at its breakfast best around the world coco s adventures vary wildly he s the face of coco pops in the uk ireland australia new zealand and loads of other countries same cheeky design same jungle vibe in the us the equivalent cereal is cocoa krispies where coco the monkey had a brief stint in the 90s before snap crackle and pop took over again elsewhere you ll find him repping choco krispies chocos or even choco krispis in places like mexico and south america sometimes with slightly tweaked looks or local twists he s a true global monkey ambassador these days coco has been a bit back benched in big uk ad campaigns as the 2000s rolled on his starring roles got quieter new spots focused more on what goes on in that bowl with only fleeting cameos and no speaking lines a lot of it ties into stricter uk consumer laws around high fat sugar and salt hfss products rules tightened to protect kids from junk food marketing making it trickier for sugary cereal mascots like coco to headline tv ads aimed at children there was even a 2018 asa drama over a coco pops granola ad that got temporarily banned for leaning too hard on the chocolatey brand vibes he s not vanished from the boxes entirely kellogg s still uses him and recent europe wide nostalgia campaigns have given him a refreshed look and brought him roaring back but the glory days of wall to wall jungle capers are a fond memory still the king of the cereal aisle in our hearts though who else grew up singing along with coco drop your favourite tweet: i use lmnt not in every glass of water throughout the day but strategically one packet in my morning water before training and sometimes a second during or after the workout depending on how much i m sweating in fort myers at 5 am the humidity alone guarantees significant sodium and potassium loss before the first working set is finished the rest of the day i drink plain water electrolyte powders make sense when you re actively losing electrolytes through sweat training in heat or eating a lower carb diet where insulin levels are lower and your kidneys excrete more sodium if you re eating a standard diet and sitting at a desk you probably don t need to add sodium to every glass of water the context matters on the bp question waking at night with drops in blood pressure is something i d bring to your physician rather than try to manage with salt alone nocturnal hypotension can have multiple causes medication timing autonomic dysfunction dehydration adrenal output patterns and the appropriate response depends on what s driving it adding salt may help if the cause is volume related but if the cause is something else you re treating a symptom without identifying the mechanism your doctor can check orthostatic vitals review medication timing and determine whether the nocturnal drops warrant further workup salt is a tool like any tool it works well when applied to the right problem but waking at night with bp drops is a symptom worth investigating not just supplementing around one more thing and this is important if i m remembering correctly that you re post thyroidectomy and on levothyroxine if so the psyllium timing i posted about isn t optional for you it s essential levothyroxine is your only source of thyroid hormone psyllium can bind to it in the gut and significantly reduce absorption take your thyroid medication first thing in the morning on an empty stomach with plain water wait at least two hours before taking psyllium if your levels have been inconsistent or your tsh has been bouncing around check whether you ve been taking anything with your levothyroxine that could be interfering with absorption fiber calcium iron coffee all of them can impact it that two hour window isn t a guideline for someone without a thyroid it s the difference between therapeutic levels and being functionally hypothyroid

tweet: i take 200mg of magnesium glycinate at night before bed but that s not my total daily magnesium intake i also get magnesium from lmnt electrolyte packets during the day and take magnesium threonate separately so my total supplemental magnesium across all sources is probably in the 350 to 400mg range daily depending on the day the rda for adult males is 400 to 420mg total 310 to 320mg for women which includes dietary sources most people get 250 to 300mg from food if they eat a reasonably whole foods diet with leafy greens nuts seeds and legumes supplementing another 200 to 400mg on top of that is a common and generally well tolerated range the reason i split the forms is they do different things glycinate at night for the calming effect on the nervous system and sleep quality threonate during the day because it crosses the blood brain barrier and is being studied for cognitive function the magnesium in lmnt is there primarily for electrolyte balance around training the practical ceiling for most people is gi tolerance if you re getting loose stools you ve exceeded your gut s absorption capacity at that dose and should back off glycinate is one of the better tolerated forms because it s chelated and doesn t draw water into the intestines the way citrate or oxide can at 200mg of glycinate alone you re unlikely to hit that threshold if you want to know where you actually stand ask for rbc magnesium on your next blood draw not serum rbc gives you a better picture of intracellular stores that tells you whether what you re taking is enough or whether you need to adjust
lda: take magnesium glycinate night bed total daily magnesium intake also get magnesium electrolyte packet day take magnesium threonate separately total supplemental magnesium across source probably range daily depending day rda adult male total woman includes dietary source people get food eat reasonably whole food diet leafy green nut seed legume supplementing another top common generally well tolerated range reason split form different thing glycinate night calming effect nervous system sleep quality threonate day cross blood brain barrier studied cognitive function magnesium primarily electrolyte balance around training practical ceiling people tolerance getting loose stool exceeded gut absorption capacity dose back glycinate one better tolerated form chelated draw water intestine way citrate oxide glycinate alone unlikely hit threshold want know actually stand ask rbc magnesium next blood draw serum rbc give better picture intracellular store tell whether taking enough whether need adjust
